free_text: Measurement and analysis of neutron spectra from thick
targets of Al and Ti bombarded by 30-50 MeV
alpha-particles
Department of physics, Visva-Bharati
university
The targets, 25 mm in diameter have been fixed
perpendicular to the beam axis.  The thickness of the
targets are such that the incident alphas are
completely stopped in the target while the scattering
and absorption of neutrons produced in the target are
negligible.
The beam energy resolution is about 200 KeV for
30-50 MeV incident alphas.
The neutrons are measured with a 52.4 mm
diameter X 2.4 mm NE-213 liquid scintillator kept at a
distance of 1.4 m from the target.  The detector angle
of acceptance is 2.14 degree and energy resolution is
about 20%.
Last checking has been done.
The total error associated with unfolded
spectra consists of
1.  The statistical error associated with the
measurement,
2.  The error arising from discretization of the
continuous spectra and of the response function,
3.  The statistical error inherent in the MONTE CARLO
calculation.
Data point reader uncertainty.
with PEGAQ, PRECO-D2, ALICE91
By CAJAD
Aluminum target thickness = 3.5 +/- 0.05 mm.
Data point reader uncertainty for 0 degree
at 30 and 40 MeV and 30 and 45 degrees at 50 MeV.
Data point reader uncertainty for 30 and 45
degrees at 30 MeV and for 0 degree for 50 MeV.

degrees at 40 MeV.
Titanium target thickness = 4.0 +/- 0.05 mm.
Enrichment is unknown.
